description: Polish and wire QuantMeet - integrate @quant/brand, wire real LiveKit backend for video conferencing, add Framer Motion spring animations, loading/empty/error/success states, responsive 360-1440px, dark mode, performance, accessibility.
steps: 1. Add @quant/brand to package.json dependencies and add path mappings to tsconfig.json
2. Update src/app/globals.css to use brand tokens. Use quantmeet app color (#EF4444) as --brand-app-color. Dark mode vars from brand dark theme
3. Update src/app/layout.tsx: import quantmeet config from @quant/brand, set brand icon, theme-color meta

4. Update src/hooks/useMeeting.ts: wire to real backend API. The backend (routes/rooms.ts) exposes room CRUD and token generation via LiveKit gateway service. Add mutations for creating rooms, joining (getting LiveKit token). Fetch room status from /api/rooms/[id]
5. Update src/hooks/useParticipants.ts: wire to real /api/rooms/[id]/participants route. Use React Query with polling or WebSocket subscription for real-time participant list updates

6. Add new hook src/hooks/useLiveKit.ts: connect to LiveKit using the token from backend. This hooks into the real LiveKit server (the backend uses livekit-server-sdk to generate tokens). Manage local tracks (audio/video), remote tracks, connection state
7. Update src/components/PreJoinLobby.tsx: device picker with camera/mic preview using real MediaDevices API. Spring animation for lobby entrance. Brand-themed UI with quantmeet colors

8. Update src/components/ParticipantGrid.tsx: real video tiles rendering LiveKit remote tracks. Spring layout animations when participants join/leave. Responsive grid (1-col on mobile, multi-col on desktop). Active speaker highlight
9. Update src/components/VideoTile.tsx: render real MediaStream video. Add connection quality indicator. Spring scale animation on speak detection. Name label with brand typography

10. Update src/components/ControlBar.tsx: brand-themed control buttons with spring microinteractions (scale on press). 44px tap targets. Proper aria labels. Responsive layout (horizontal on desktop, compact on mobile)
11. Update src/components/ChatPanel.tsx: wire to real WebSocket chat via backend /ws route. Spring slide-in panel animation. Loading/empty states
12. Update src/components/ParticipantList.tsx: real participant data from LiveKit. Spring stagger for list items
13. Update src/components/MeetingEnded.tsx: brand-themed meeting summary. Spring entrance animation
14. Update src/app/page.tsx (home): brand logo, recent meetings from real API, spring animations

15. Update src/app/meeting/[id]/page.tsx: full meeting page wired to LiveKit. Handle connection states (connecting/connected/disconnected/failed). Show LoadingState during connection, ErrorState on failure
16. Ensure responsive 360-1440px, dark mode, 44px tap targets, proper keyboard navigation
17. Run pnpm --filter @quant/quantmeet typecheck && pnpm --filter @quant/quantmeet lint to verify
acceptance_criteria: pnpm --filter @quant/quantmeet typecheck passes with zero errors
pnpm --filter @quant/quantmeet lint passes with zero errors
@quant/brand is imported and used (motion springs, app config, theme colors)
LiveKit integration wired to real backend (token generation via backend, connect to LiveKit server)
Real MediaDevices API used for camera/mic in lobby
React Query for room info and participant data
Framer Motion spring animations on participant grid, control bar, panels
Dark mode fully functional with brand dark theme colors
All interactive elements have 44px minimum tap targets and proper aria attributes
